turnaround_reason: Stock has cratered from $34 to $12.5 (63% below 52-week high) after a Q1 2026 revenue decline of 9% and a $249M impairment charge on the Atkins/Quest brands. Management lowered FY26 EBITDA guidance by up to 22% citing commodity inflation and tariffs. The thesis is that the impairment is a kitchen-sink reset, forward P/E is ~7x, and the company still generates $125M FCF with a viable snack portfolio.
bullish_points: Forward P/E of ~7x and EV/EBITDA of ~6x — very cheap if earnings stabilize
$125M FCF on $1.16B market cap = ~11% FCF yield, strong cash generation
Price-to-book of 0.76 — trading below liquidation value of net assets
Director insider purchases: Kilts bought $991K at $12.39 in April, Daley bought $118K at $11.78 in May — directors putting real money in at these levels
Q2 2026 earnings beat estimates ($0.45 vs $0.40 est, +13.6%) suggesting the worst may be behind them
Current ratio of 5.06 — pristine balance sheet, no liquidity risk
bearish_points: Revenue declining 9.4% YoY — the Atkins/Quest brands are losing shelf relevance
$249M impairment signals permanent value destruction in brand intangibles
Tariff and commodity inflation pressure may persist through 2026
Profit margins negative at -7.5% — a consumer staples company losing money is a red flag
No clear catalyst for revenue re-acceleration; diet/snack category is crowded and competitive
Screener news data was all null — yfinance may have pulled incomplete data, confidence limited
catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings on July 9, 2026 — if revenue decline narrows and management reaffirms or raises the lowered EBITDA guidance, the re-rating could be sharp given the low multiple
suggested_trade: Spot only — small position. The value trap risk is high with declining revenue. Wait for July 9 earnings before considering larger size.
key_risk: Revenue continues to decline and brand impairment proves the business is structurally impaired, not cyclical — a value trap at any price
would_skip_if: Revenue decline accelerates beyond -10% or management cuts guidance again at Q3 earnings
